latest.title: MENA, GCC and Asia Marketing Agreement
latest.content:
2 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8138U
Powerhouse Energy Group PLC
02 March 2026
2nd March 2026
Powerhouse Energy Group plc
("Powerhouse" or the "Company")
MENA, GCC and Asia Marketing Agreement
Abraham Accord linked company to market PHE in MENA, GCC and Asia
Powerhouse Energy Group plc (AIM: PHE),?a company pioneering integrated technology that converts non-recyclable waste into low carbon energy with its revenue generating engineering consulting subsidiary ("Engsolve"), is pleased to announce that it has signed a marketing agreement with Green Gecko Energy Limited ("Green Gecko"), a UK based consultancy, which will act as the Company's sole agent in introducing Powerhouse to potential opportunities across the Abraham Accord countries, the wider MENA and GCC region, and certain countries within Asia.
Highlights:
·
Green Gecko was formed by David Scott and Peter Nutt and in the past 12 months they, and Sir Liam Fox, who is an adviser to the company, have been working in the Abraham Accords signatory countries, the wider MENA and GCC region and certain countries within Asia to identify a number of projects to exclusively utilise the Powerhouse high-temperature pyrolysis (HTP) modular solution.
·
Projects currently identified for Powerhouse by the Green Gecko team are located in Bahrain, Egypt, Kuwait and Oman; and address market niches including:
o
pilot sites, predominately to address the processing of tyres and other elastomers and/or polymers as well as plastic(s); and
o
deployment of high-temperature pyrolysis (HTP) modular solutions, which can be expanded as performance and offtake markets are proven.
·
No up-front funding is required from the Company.
·
Agreement is on a "no win, no fee" basis, where any payment by the Company to Green Gecko will be made on a commission basis, and satisfied through a combination of cash and options over up to 3% of the Company's current share capital (exercisable at a price of 0.5 pence) wherein specific construction related milestones (and associated payments to PHE) are achieved by the projects introduced by Green Gecko.
Commenting on the Agreement, Paul Emmitt, Powerhouse CEO, said:
"We are delighted to enter into this agreement with the Green Gecko team. MENA is a huge market, and our technology has the potential to provide a solution to the green energy needs of this region. David and Peter are highly connected and experienced in the area and we believe that with them we will gain significant traction for our business model and offering.
"We look forward to working with the Green Gecko team in further spreading our geographic footprint as this relationship delivers the opportunities that we believe are inherent in the region for our technology."
Peter Nutt and David Scott, Directors of Green Gecko Energy said:
"We both are delighted to enter into this agreement with Powerhouse Energy.? We have a number of projects which we anticipate bringing to the Company from our relationships in the Abraham Accord countries, MENA, GCC region and Asia.?
"We look forward to working with Paul and the Team in the year ahead."
About Green Gecko Energy
Green Gecko Energy is a private limited company, incorporated last year, founded by David Scott and Peter Nutt with a strategy to market Powerhouse's technology and model in the Abraham Accord Countries, MENA and the wider GCC region and Asia.
David Scott has over 40 years of experience in corporate broking and investment banking industry, advising small and medium-sized companies listed on the London Stock Exchange and AIM Market. He has extensive expertise in private equity, mergers and acquisitions, and strategic advisory work for investors and management teams in the UK and internationally.
He currently serves as a Partner at Chayton Capital LLP, an FCA-regulated investment firm, and CEO of the Abraham Accords Prosperity Group Limited, which is currently developing three businesses, (i) Prosperity Microgrid Partners, which will develop the Green Gecko opportunity, (ii) Prosperity Reach Partners, which is focused on delivering service sector solutions, initially in Bahrain and Morocco and (iii) a fund, focused on creating employment opportunities for young people and businesses in the GCC Region.
Peter Nutt has many decades experience in corporate finance advising and arranging experience having worked from bases in the City of London, Sydney, Australia and twice from Tokyo, Japan. He is very familiar with complex cross-border financing structures having arranged many lease and debt transactions, particularly in the aviation sectors international flag carriers. This knowledge coupled with senior contacts around the world will be used for the benefit of Green Gecko.
-ENDS-
For more information, please visit
www.phegroup.com
or contact:
Powerhouse Energy Group Plc
Paul Emmitt, CEO
Via Tavistock below
Ben Brier, CFO
Strand Hanson Limited
(Nominated & Financial Adviser and Joint Broker)
+44 (0) 207 409 3494
Ritchie Balmer / Rob Patrick / James Harris
Longspur Capital Limited
(Financial Adviser & Joint Broker)
+44 (0) 203 940 6608
Adam Robertson
Tavistock
(Financial PR)
+44 (0) 207 920 3150
Simon Hudson / Nick Elwes / Saskia Sizen
powerhouse@tavistock.co.uk
About Powerhouse Energy Group Plc
Powerhouse Energy has developed a process technology which can utilise waste plastic, end-of-life-tyres, and other waste streams to convert them efficiently and economically into syngas from which valuable products such as chemical precursors, hydrogen, electricity, heat and other industrial products may be derived. PHE's process produces low levels of safe residues and requires a small operating footprint, making it suitable for deployment at enterprise and community level.
Powerhouse also incorporates Engsolve Ltd, which is a revenue generating business offering Engineering Services across all sectors, with speciality services in the development of new technologies and clean energy.

a company pioneering integrated technology that converts non-recyclable waste into low carbon energy with a revenue generating engineering consulting division ("Engsolve")

Extensive design and engineering work has already been completed to enable the submission and below are some renderings of the proposed facility which are included in the planning application.

latest.title: Signing of Agreement to Lease Site in Ballymena
latest.content:
26 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2379Q
Powerhouse Energy Group PLC
26 January 2026
26 January 2026
Powerhouse Energy Group plc
("Powerhouse" or the "Company")
Signing of Agreement to Lease Site in Ballymena
Powerhouse Energy Group plc (AIM: PHE),
a company pioneering integrated technology that converts non-recyclable waste into low carbon energy with a revenue generating engineering consulting division ("Engsolve")
,
is pleased to announce that it has signed an agreement to lease (the "Lease Agreement") for a 1.98 acre serviced site at Silverwood Business Park, Ballymena, Northern Ireland.
Terms of the Lease Agreement:
·
Site owner: Silverwood Business Park Ltd.
·
Site address: Silverwood Business Park, Ballymena, County Antrim.
·
Annual rent:
An initial rent-free period of up to 12 months and, thereafter, subject to five yearly reviews.
·
Length of agreement:
Lease is for a 25-year term.
·
Break clause:
A tenant break clause on the fifth anniversary of term commencement.
Powerhouse plans to build, own and operate a 40 tonnes per day (TPD) waste to hydrogen facility at the site. The facility is intended take 40 TPD of waste destined for landfill and convert it into 99.999% pure hydrogen for a variety of uses, including transport. Discussions are already taking place on feedstock and offtake agreements.
The Company will shortly submit the required planning application to the Mid and East Antrim Borough Council, and a further announcement will be made once this has been submitted. Approval of the planning application is a condition to the Lease Agreement becoming effective.
Commenting on the Lease Agreement, Paul Emmitt, Powerhouse CEO, said:
"This is a major step forward for the Company. Ballymena is set to become the Hydrogen Hub of Northern Ireland, with the organisations including Wrightbus, Translink and the Mid and East Antrim Borough Council all focused on developing the hydrogen economy in Northern Ireland."
-ENDS-
For more information, please visit

latest.title: Australian Project update
latest.content:
1 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 5141J
Powerhouse Energy Group PLC
01 December 2025
1 December 2025
Powerhouse Energy Group Plc
("Powerhouse", "PHE" or the "Company")
Australian Project update
Powerhouse (AIM:PHE), a company pioneering integrated technology that converts non-recyclable waste into low carbon energy together with a revenue generating engineering consulting division (Engsolve), is pleased to provide the following update regarding its Framework Agreement with National Hydrogen of Australia (NH2).
Further to the Company's announcement dated 30 June 2025, NH2 has now established the project SPV "National Waste to Energy", in which PHE will hold a 5% equity stake, in return for support the Company is providing to NH2 (and the SPV) to take FID on its Perth project. As part of working towards this, NH2 has also received a signed Offer to Lease from the Western Australian Government for up to 50 years, for a strategically located and appropriately zoned site which is large enough to accommodate two of Powerhouse's patented Distributed Modular Generation ("DMG") trains in the Rockingham Strategic Industrial Area, just south of Perth.
The project being delivered is expected to be undertaken in a phased approach as outlined below:
·
Phase 1 - Construction of a single ~40 Tonne per Day ("TPD") train DMG producing power
·
Phase 2 - the initial ~40 TPD single train will have a pre-deigned Hydrogen separation and compression unit added to allow Hydrogen production as the hydrogen market grows for heavy transport solutions
·
Phase 3 - Construction of a second ~40 TPD DMG train.
National Waste to Energy has also completed a power offtake agreement with the largest independent commercial energy provider in Perth and wider Western Australia. In addition, National Waste to Energy has secured a feedstock agreement for sorted mixed plastics with a company that has very significant experience in the waste management business.
PHE are also supporting NH2 with the provision of technical documentation such as Patents, FEED package, license agreements etc, for a large UK based, international commercial finance company currently undertaking financial due diligence on the project.
As this is a client driven project, the Company is working to their timescales and will provide further updates as they are available.
Paul Emmitt, Chief Executive Officer of Powerhouse, commented:
"The creation of the SPV, the signed Offer to Lease and the progression of funding are significant steps forward in our collaboration with National Hydrogen and a further huge endorsement of our DMG process. We are delighted to be an incoming shareholder in the SPV; working on this project with National Hydrogen, as well as further building on our relationship with them and supporting them to deliver other projects."
For more information, please visit
www.phegroup.com
or contact:
Powerhouse Energy Group Plc

company_30s.what_they_do: PHE.L is a renewable‑energy developer that builds and operates hydrogen production plants. Their core offering is green hydrogen generated from low‑carbon electricity, which they supply to industrial users and fuel‑cell fleets.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Hydrogen is becoming a cornerstone of the low‑carbon economy, especially for heavy‑duty transport, steelmaking and power‑to‑gas. PHE is positioned to tap a growing market that governments and corporates are actively supporting with subsidies and procurement contracts.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in the development phase. It has secured site leases and planning permissions but has not yet begun construction or generated revenue. Cash burn is ongoing, and the company is raising capital to fund the next stage of its pipeline.

rns_narrative: The company is moving through the early stages of a hydrogen project in Ballymena – a lease has been signed and a planning application submitted.
Financially, PHE has completed a £1.25 m placing and is preparing for a larger equity raise, signalling an effort to shore up working capital.
Operational updates highlight progress on a feed‑stock testing unit and a letter of intent for an offtake agreement, suggesting that the project is edging closer to a commercial launch.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence around PHE, but hydrogen buzz is alive elsewhere
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No specific bullish chatter about PHE has surfaced; traders are more focused on larger hydrogen names like IQE or HDD.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: The lack of visible progress and the heavy capital requirement raise concerns about execution risk and the possibility of cost overruns.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has remained largely indifferent; any sudden news could ignite a quick spike, but there is no sustained optimism or pessimism yet.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows zero interest – a clear sign that any future announcement could catch the market off‑guard.

near_term_catalysts: Approval of the Ballymena planning application, which would allow construction to begin.
A formal offtake agreement that locks in a buyer for the first batch of hydrogen.
A successful capital raise that provides the cash needed to move from design to build.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to watch in the next two to three months are a volume spike on a news day, a price break above the current resistance near the planning approval level, and the official signing of the offtake contract.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the pre‑crash, pre‑recovery phase. There is no evidence of a 70‑95 % drop, so the mean‑reversion model does not yet apply. A clear catalyst is required before any upside can be considered.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the company lacks a deep crash and the next move depends on regulatory and financing milestones.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the planning approval is granted, the offtake deal is signed, and the capital raise closes, the company could start production and unlock a new revenue stream. This would lift the stock into a new growth trajectory.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Delays in permits, cost overruns, or a failed funding round could push the firm into a liquidity crisis. The lack of current revenue means any upside is contingent on a successful transition to production.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is capped by the size of the project pipeline and the need for further capital, while downside risk is high because the firm is still in development and could fail to secure the necessary approvals or financing.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only after a clear catalyst – for example, a signed offtake contract or a successful capital raise. Position sizing should be conservative (5–10 % of discretionary capital) and a stop set just below the recent low of the planning‑approval support level.
playbook.if_watching: Monitor the stock for a volume surge on a news day. If the price breaches the next resistance level and volume exceeds 1.5× the average, consider a small entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to secure planning approval or a capital raise within the next 90 days, consider walking away; the risk of prolonged cash burn outweighs any speculative upside.

ai_insights.bottom_line: PHE is still in the early development stage with no deep crash yet; a decisive regulatory or financing milestone is needed before any upside can materialise, and the risk remains high.
